back_translation: While Jesus was passing by, He saw a certain man who had been blind from birth.
His disciples asked Him, “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?”
Jesus answered, “Neither this man sinned, nor his parents, but the reason this is so, is that the works of God may be displayed in him.
We must do the works of the One who sent Me, while it is still day. Night is coming, when no one can work.
While I am still in this world, I am the light of the world.”
When He had said this, He spat saliva on the ground, then made mud out of the saliva, and applied the mud to the eyes of that man,
and said to him, “Go and wash in the pool of Siloam” (which is interpreted, ‘the One who has been sent’). So that man went and washed, and came back able to see.
His neighbors and those who had previously seen him begging said, “Is this not the man who used to sit and beg?”
Some said, “Yes, this is the one.” Others said, “No, only the face is similar.” But that man kept insisting, “It is I myself.”
So they asked him, “Then how were your eyes opened?”
He answered, “The man who is called Jesus made mud, applied it to my eyes, and said to me, ‘Go to Siloam and wash.’ So I went and washed, and then I could see.”
They asked him, “Where is that one?” He answered, “I do not know.”
They brought the man who had once been blind to the Pharisees.
The day on which Jesus had made the mud and opened his eyes was a Sabbath.
So the Pharisees asked him again how he had come to see. He answered them, “He put mud on my eyes, then I washed, and now I can see.”
Some of the Pharisees therefore said, “This man is not from God, because he does not keep the Sabbath.” But others said, “How can a sinner perform such signs?” And there was division among them.
They asked the blind man again, “What do you say about him, since he opened your eyes?” He answered, “That one is a prophet.”
The Jewish leaders did not yet believe about him that he had been blind and had received sight, until they summoned the parents of the one who received sight to come to them.
They then asked the parents, “Is this your son, the one whom you say was born blind? So how is it that he can see now?”
His parents therefore answered, “We know that he is our son, and that he was born blind.
But how he can see now, we do not know, and who opened his eyes, we also do not know. Ask him yourselves. He is already grown to adulthood; he can speak about his own matter.”
His parents said this because they feared the Jewish leaders, for the Jewish leaders had already agreed that if anyone confessed Jesus to be the Christ, that one would be expelled from the synagogue.
For this reason his parents said, “He is already grown to adulthood; ask him yourselves.”
So they called the man who had once been blind a second time, and said to him, “Give glory to God. We know that that man is a sinner.”
He therefore answered, “Whether that one is a sinner or not, I do not know. One thing I do know is that I was once blind, but now I can see.”
They then asked him, “What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?”
He answered them, “I have already told you, but you would not listen. Why do you want to hear again? Or do you also want to become disciples of that one?”
They then reviled him, saying, “You are a disciple of that one, but we are disciples of Moses.
We know that God has spoken to Moses, but as for this one, we do not know where he comes from.”
That man therefore answered them, “This is what is remarkable — that you do not know where he comes from, and yet he was able to open my eyes.
We know that God does not listen to sinners, but if anyone fears God and does His will, He listens to that one.
From ancient times until now, it has never been heard that anyone has opened the eyes of one born blind.
If this man were not from God, he could not do anything like this.”
They therefore answered him, “You were born wholly in sins, and you would teach us?” And they drove him out.
Jesus knew that they had driven him out, and so He sought him out and found him, and said, “Do you believe in the Son of Man?”
He answered, “Esteemed sir, who is that one, that I may believe in him?”
Jesus said to him, “You have already seen that one, and the One who is now speaking with you, that very one is He.”
He said reverently, “My Lord, I believe.” And he worshiped Him.
Jesus then said, “I came into this world for judgment, so that those who do not see may see, and those who see may become blind.”
Some Pharisees who were with Him heard this and asked Him reverently, “Are we also blind?”
Jesus said to them, “If you were blind, you would have no sin. But since you say, ‘We see,’ your sin still remains.
